proposed_hypotheses.text: Older age_years is associated with lower probability of objective_response in this AML cohort.
Female patients (sex_female=1) have a different objective_response rate than males (sex_female=0).
Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response (worse performance status worsens response).
Patients with secondary_aml=1 have lower objective_response than de novo AML patients (secondary_aml=0).
Patients flagged unfit_for_intensive=1 have a different objective_response rate than fit patients (unfit_for_intensive=0).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for demographics, comorbidity flags, mutations and labs, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine=1 retains an independent positive association with objective_response.

After adjustment for the same covariates, treatment_midostaurin, treatment_gilteritinib, treatment_ivosidenib, treatment_enasidenib, and treatment_7plus3 each have NO independent main-effect association with objective_response.
After adjustment for confounders, the unfit_for_intensive=1 main effect on objective_response BECOMES POSITIVE (sign flips), suggesting venetoclax-azacitidine confounding.
Adjusted positive effect of npm1_mutation=1 on objective_response remains.
Adjusted negative effect of tp53_mutation=1 on objective_response remains.
Adjusted negative effect of higher ecog_ps on objective_response remains.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In flt3_itd=1 patients, treatment_midostaurin=1 is associated with HIGHER objective_response than treatment_midostaurin=0; the treatment_midostaurin × flt3_itd interaction term in a logistic model with covariates is positive and significant.
In flt3_itd=1 patients, treatment_gilteritinib=1 is associated with HIGHER objective_response; logit interaction term treatment_gilteritinib:flt3_itd is positive.
In flt3_tkd=1 patients, treatment_gilteritinib=1 is associated with HIGHER objective_response; logit interaction term treatment_gilteritinib:flt3_tkd is positive.
In idh1_mutation=1 patients, treatment_ivosidenib=1 is associated with HIGHER objective_response; logit interaction term treatment_ivosidenib:idh1_mutation is positive.
In idh2_mutation=1 patients, treatment_enasidenib=1 is associated with HIGHER objective_response; logit interaction term treatment_enasidenib:idh2_mutation is positive.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response is LARGER in unfit_for_intensive=1 than in unfit_for_intensive=0 (treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × unfit_for_intensive interaction is positive).
The treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response is LARGER in npm1_mutation=1 than in npm1_mutation=0 (treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × npm1_mutation interaction is positive).
The treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response is SMALLER (or absent) in tp53_mutation=1 than in tp53_mutation=0 (interaction term is negative).
The treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response is SMALLER in complex_karyotype=1 than in complex_karyotype=0 (interaction term is negative).
The treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × age_years interaction on objective_response is non-zero (effect size varies with age).
The treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response is similar across sex_female and secondary_aml strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_7plus3 effect on objective_response declines with older age_years (treatment_7plus3 × age_years interaction is negative).
The treatment_7plus3 effect on objective_response is smaller in unfit_for_intensive=1 than fit patients.
The treatment_7plus3 effect on objective_response is smaller in complex_karyotype=1 than complex_karyotype=0 (negative interaction).
The treatment_7plus3 effect on objective_response varies by npm1_mutation status.
The treatment_7plus3 × {flt3_itd, flt3_tkd, idh1, idh2, tp53, secondary_aml, sex_female, ecog_ps} interactions are not significant.
Within flt3_itd=1, treatment_midostaurin=1 confers a positive response benefit specifically in npm1_mutation=1 patients (and possibly nowhere else).
Combining FLT3 mutations (flt3_itd=1 OR flt3_tkd=1), treatment_gilteritinib=1 yields HIGHER objective_response than treatment_gilteritinib=0 in this FLT3-mutated subgroup.
Within idh1_mutation=1, treatment_ivosidenib=1 confers a positive benefit at least in some sub-subset (e.g., npm1_mutation=0, tp53_mutation=0, unfit_for_intensive=1).
Within idh2_mutation=1, treatment_enasidenib=1 confers a positive benefit at least in some sub-subset (e.g., unfit_for_intensive=1).
In tp53_mutation=1 patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has NO objective_response benefit (rate diff ≈ 0); the benefit is concentrated in tp53_mutation=0 patients.
Even within tp53_mutation=0, the venetoclax-azacitidine response benefit is further attenuated when complex_karyotype=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine objective_response benefit is concentrated specifically in patients meeting the conjunction (unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0) (the "VEN-AZA supergroup"); outside this subgroup the benefit is zero.

In the simpler subgroup (unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1), the venetoclax-azacitidine effect on objective_response is already very large; further restricting to tp53_mutation=0 and complex_karyotype=0 increases it further.
In unfit_for_intensive=1 but npm1_mutation=0 patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has NO objective_response benefit.
In npm1_mutation=1 but unfit_for_intensive=0 patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has NO objective_response benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The triple-interaction term treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × unfit_for_intensive × npm1_mutation in a logistic regression of objective_response (with main effects, two-way interactions, and other covariates) is positive and highly significant — formally confirming the synergistic subgroup structure observed in stratified analyses.

In the full triple-interaction model, the marginal treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine main effect (i.e., for unfit=0, npm1=0) is no longer significant — the apparent overall main effect arose entirely from the unfit=1 ∩ npm1=1 subgroup.

analyses.code: logit y ~ venaza*unfit*npm1 + other covs; triple-interaction term

proposed_hypotheses.text: The non-VEN-AZA treatments (treatment_midostaurin, treatment_gilteritinib, treatment_ivosidenib, treatment_enasidenib, treatment_7plus3) have NO objective_response benefit even when stratified by unfit_for_intensive.
The treatment_7plus3 effect on objective_response is similar across age tertiles (no age-driven heterogeneity).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across the 6 treatments × 10 binary feature combinations, the treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × {npm1_mutation, unfit_for_intensive} interactions remain by far the largest positive (and tp53/complex_karyotype the largest negative) — a comprehensive interaction screen confirms VEN-AZA is the dominant heterogeneous-effect treatment.

Targeted treatments (mido, gilt, ivo, ena) and 7+3 produce no major significant Tx × marker interactions in this systematic screen (apart from 7+3:complex_karyotype negative and a couple of marginal/spurious associations).

analyses.code: loop logit y ~ tx*feat + covs over treatments × binary features
midostaurin × flt3_itd interaction (the textbook target — should be ZERO here)

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × blast_pct_marrow interaction on objective_response is positive (effect attenuates less, or even amplifies, at higher marrow blast burden).
Other continuous-lab × treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine interactions (wbc_k_per_ul, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, crp_mg_l, nlr, hemoglobin_g_dl) on objective_response are not significant.

All four predicates of the VEN-AZA supergroup definition (unfit_for_intensive=1, npm1_mutation=1, tp53_mutation=0, complex_karyotype=0) are necessary: violating any single predicate (e.g., flipping unfit→0 OR npm1→0 OR tp53→1 OR complex_karyotype→1) eliminates the venetoclax-azacitidine objective_response benefit.

Dropping a single predicate from the supergroup definition (e.g., {npm1=1, tp53=0, ck=0} without the unfit predicate) yields a smaller (but still positive) average venetoclax-azacitidine effect, because the dropped subgroup includes some NPM1+ patients who are not unfit and where VEN-AZA does not work.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In complex_karyotype=1 patients, treatment_7plus3=1 is associated with LOWER objective_response than treatment_7plus3=0 (paradoxical/futility signal in adverse cytogenetics).
In tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0 patients (favorable cytogenetics), treatment_7plus3=1 is associated with marginally HIGHER objective_response than treatment_7plus3=0.

Within flt3_itd=1 patients, treatment_midostaurin=1 has NO independent positive effect on objective_response after adjusting for age, ECOG, and other key predictors (i.e., the textbook FLT3-targeting indication is not detectable in this dataset).
Within flt3_itd=1 patients, treatment_gilteritinib=1 has NO independent positive effect on objective_response after adjustment.
Within idh1_mutation=1 patients, treatment_ivosidenib=1 has NO positive (and possibly slightly negative) adjusted effect on objective_response.
Within idh2_mutation=1 patients, treatment_enasidenib=1 has NO independent positive effect on objective_response after adjustment.

analyses.code: subset flt3_itd=1: logit y ~ mido + age + ecog + npm1 + tp53 + ck
analyses.result_summary: within flt3_itd=1: adjusted logit beta(treatment_midostaurin)=-0.1016, p=0.172

analyses.code: subset flt3_itd=1: logit y ~ gilt + age + ecog + npm1 + tp53 + ck
analyses.result_summary: within flt3_itd=1: adjusted logit beta(treatment_gilteritinib)=0.0205, p=0.776

analyses.code: subset idh1=1: logit y ~ ivo + age + ecog + npm1 + tp53 + ck + unfit
analyses.result_summary: within idh1=1: adjusted logit beta(treatment_ivosidenib)=-0.3092, p=0.0664

analyses.code: subset idh2=1: logit y ~ ena + age + ecog + npm1 + tp53 + ck + unfit
analyses.result_summary: within idh2=1: adjusted logit beta(treatment_enasidenib)=0.0182, p=0.898

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the (unfit_for_intensive=1 ∩ npm1_mutation=1) subgroup, the treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response does NOT vary materially with age_years (no further age-driven heterogeneity).
Within the same subgroup, the treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × tp53_mutation interaction on objective_response is strongly NEGATIVE — TP53 mutation acts as a within-subgroup suppressor.
Within the same subgroup, the treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × complex_karyotype interaction on objective_response is strongly NEGATIVE — complex karyotype acts as a within-subgroup suppressor.

analyses.code: subset unfit=1 & npm1=1: logit y ~ venaza*age + tp53 + ck + ecog + albumin
subset unfit=1 & npm1=1: logit y ~ venaza*tp53 + ck + ecog + albumin
subset unfit=1 & npm1=1: logit y ~ venaza*ck + tp53 + ecog + albumin

proposed_hypotheses.text: In npm1_mutation=1 patients, treatment_ivosidenib=1 is associated with LOWER objective_response than treatment_ivosidenib=0 (i.e., the marginal ivosidenib × npm1 interaction observed in the screen reflects a small but real negative ivosidenib effect in NPM1+ patients).

The marginal enasidenib × sex_female interaction reflects directionally opposite stratified effects: enasidenib lowers response in males (sex_female=0) and raises it in females (sex_female=1) — but both effect sizes are small and likely not clinically meaningful.

analyses.code: subset npm1=1: ivosidenib on vs off
subset npm1=0: ivosidenib on vs off
subset sex_female=0: enasidenib on vs off
subset sex_female=1: enasidenib on vs off

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a logistic regression of objective_response on treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × supergroup (where supergroup = (unfit_for_intensive=1 & npm1_mutation=1 & tp53_mutation=0 & complex_karyotype=0)) plus other patient covariates, the venetoclax-azacitidine × supergroup interaction is positive and the venetoclax-azacitidine main effect (in the non-supergroup) is essentially zero — confirming the supergroup definition formally.

analyses.code: logit y ~ venaza*supergroup + remaining covs (age, sex, ecog, secondary_aml, FLT3, IDH, labs)

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine objective_response benefit is similar in secondary_aml=1 and secondary_aml=0 (no secondary-AML modifier, in contrast to npm1 / tp53 / complex_karyotype / unfit_for_intensive).

analyses.code: subset secondary_aml=0 venaza on vs off
subset secondary_aml=1 venaza on vs off

proposed_hypotheses.text: In complex_karyotype=0 patients, after adjustment for age, ECOG, fitness, key mutations and labs, treatment_7plus3=1 has at most a small positive (and statistically marginal) effect on objective_response.

analyses.code: subset complex_karyotype=0: logit y ~ 7+3 + age + ecog + unfit + npm1 + tp53 + secondary + albumin + blast_pct
analyses.result_summary: within complex_karyotype=0: adjusted logit beta(treatment_7plus3)=0.0456, p=0.0691

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the supergroup (unfit_for_intensive=1 ∩ npm1_mutation=1 ∩ tp53_mutation=0 ∩ complex_karyotype=0), the adjusted log-odds-ratio for treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine on objective_response is large positive (≥ +2.5; OR ≥ ~12), with very small p-value, after controlling for age, sex, ECOG, secondary AML, FLT3/IDH mutations, and key labs.

analyses.code: subset supergroup=1: logit y ~ venaza + age + ecog + sex + secondary + flt3_itd + flt3_tkd + idh1 + idh2 + albumin + blast_pct + crp
analyses.result_summary: within supergroup (unfit=1&npm1=1&tp53=0&ck=0): adjusted logit beta(treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine)=3.0365, OR=20.833, p=0, n=4531

proposed_hypotheses.text: Outside the supergroup (i.e., in the complement, n≈45,000), the adjusted treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response is essentially ZERO (|β| < 0.05 on the logit scale, p > 0.4) — final negative-control confirmation that the entire VEN-AZA benefit is contained inside the (unfit_for_intensive=1 ∩ npm1_mutation=1 ∩ tp53_mutation=0 ∩ complex_karyotype=0) supergroup.

FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP CALL: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine improves objective_response substantially and exclusively in the subgroup defined by (unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0) — response 78.6% on vs 15.8% off (Δ ≈ +62.8 percentage points, adjusted OR ≈ 21). Outside this subgroup the effect is null. The two suppressors are tp53_mutation=1 and complex_karyotype=1; the two required positive predicates are unfit_for_intensive=1 and npm1_mutation=1.

analyses.code: subset supergroup=0 (n≈45469): logit y ~ venaza + all covariates
analyses.result_summary: outside supergroup: adjusted logit beta(treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine)=-0.0187, OR=0.981, p=0.47, n=45469

analyses.code: Final supergroup vs complement summary: see iter 24/25 adjusted ORs above
analyses.result_summary: Summary: within supergroup adjusted log-OR(treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine)=+3.04 (OR≈21, p≈0); outside supergroup adjusted log-OR=-0.019 (OR≈0.98, p=0.47). The subgroup-treatment interaction is unambiguous: VEN-AZA helps only in (unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0).
